business.headline: Air India fares to rise as Iran war pushes up jet fuel pricesAir India said the fuel surcharge is due to the sharp rise in aviation turbine fuel (ATF) prices since early March 2026, triggered by supply disruptions in the Gulf region.

G7 stops short of releasing oil reserves, asks IEA to assess market optionsAfter soaring to a more than three-year high in the previous session, oil prices plummeted 7 per cent on Tuesday as US President Donald Trump predicted the war in the Middle East could end soon, easing concerns about prolonged disruptions to oil supplies.

India eases FDI rules for China, allows up to 10% stake without govt approvalUnder the revised policy, overseas investors with up to 10% beneficial ownership from countries sharing a land border with India will now be allowed to invest through the automatic route.

Main Hoon Naa: IndiGo interim head's message to employees amid leadership changeRahul Bhatia expressed gratitude to employees who worked tirelessly to restore operations during that challenging period, praising their resilience and dedication.

IndiGo CEO Pieter Elbers steps down 3 months after operational meltdownThe resignation comes after the airline faced its worst-ever operational crisis in December, which drew scrutiny from both the public and the government.

Airlines raise fares as Iran conflict sends jet fuel prices soaringThe Iran-US conflict has disrupted shipping through a key global oil export route, pushing oil prices higher. As a result, airline tickets on some routes have become much more expensive, and there are growing concerns that travel demand could weaken if the situation continues.

No LPG crisis, production up 10% today, says government, warns against fake newsGovernment sources said oil refineries have increased LPG production by around 10% following directions issued earlier and that additional administrative measures have been put in place to prevent hoarding and malpractice.

Middle East countries cut daily oil output amid Hormuz disruptionFour of the region's largest producers, including Saudi Arabia, Iraq, the United Arab Emirates and Kuwait have collectively reduced output by as much as 6.7 million barrels per day.
Sensex closes 640 points higher, Nifty above 24,200; M&M gains 4%The S&P BSE Sensex jumped 639.82 points to close at 78,205.98, while the NSE Nifty50 added 233.55 points to end at 24,261.60.

Essential Commodities Act invoked by India amid LPG shortage. What does it mean?Restaurants, hotels and commercial kitchens in cities such as Delhi, Mumbai and Bengaluru have reported difficulty getting commercial LPG cylinders, forcing some establishments to scale down operations or temporarily shut. The situation has pushed the government to invoke the Essential Commodities Act

Silver jumps nearly Rs 10,000: Will the Iran-US war push prices even higher?Geopolitical shocks are once again driving money into precious metals. Silver has surged nearly Rs 10,000 amid rising tensions between the US, Israel and Iran. As uncertainty deepens, the big question now is: can silver rally even further?

Prolonged Gulf conflict could be catastrophic for oil markets, says Aramco chiefSpeaking during a media call after the company released its 2025 financial results, Saudi Aramco CEO Amin Nasser said the disruption represents the most serious crisis faced by the region's oil and gas sector in recent years.

EPF rate stays at 8.25%: Should investors continue with VPF or choose SIPs?For many salaried employees, EPF and Voluntary Provident Fund (VPF) are trusted tools for retirement savings. But with the EPF rate staying at 8.25%, some investors are wondering if they should keep contributing to VPF or move extra savings into SIPs for better long-term returns?

Highlights: No need to panic, India expands oil sourcing to 40 countries, say Govt sourcesLPG Shortage Highlights: A growing LPG cylinder shortage is disrupting restaurants and hotels across several Indian cities, raising concerns over a widening gas supply crisis. Hospitality associations have warned that eateries are struggling to procure commercial LPG cylinders, forcing some establishments to shut operations temporarily. The shortage is being reported in cities such as Mumbai, Bengaluru and Kolkata, where restaurant bodies say supplies have become irregular over the past few days. The government has asked oil companies and refineries to increase LPG production to stabilise supply. Track this live blog for the latest updates on the LPG cylinder shortage, city-wise impact on restaurants, supply disruptions, and steps being taken to address the crisis.

When crude oil hit a record $147 per barrel in 2008 and the reason was not warCrude oil briefly touched $120 amid Gulf tensions, reviving memories of the historic 2008 oil shock when prices hit $147. But unlike this conflict, the 2008 surge was not driven by war or geopolitics. So what pushed oil prices so high? Read on to find out.

Why have crude oil prices fallen below $100, and will it go up again?Crude oil prices had surged above $100 per barrel on Monday but fell today after recent comments from US President Donald Trump. Experts say that even though prices have fallen today, the risk to global oil supply has not gone away.

LPG shortage shuts 20% of Mumbai hotels and restaurants  Live UpdatesThe association warned that up to 50% of hotels in the city could shut down within the next two days if the gas supply situation does not improve.
Sensex jumps 500 points, Nifty above 24,100; IndiGo up 4%The S&P BSE Sensex added 379.79 points to 77,945.95, while the NSE Nifty50 jumped 106.40 points to 24,134.45 as of 9:28 am.

Sensex, Nifty opening: Will stock market crash again or open higher today?Markets have been under pressure in recent days due to the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. Last week, both benchmark indices, the Sensex and the Nifty, recorded their worst weekly performance in more than a year.

Why oil controls the world economyOil has shaped wars, alliances and global economies for more than a century. A sudden disruption in supply can shake financial markets, push up inflation and send governments scrambling for solutions.

entertainment.headline: Our missing child: Supriya Pathak on her bond with Shahid Kapoor's wife Mira Rajput Supriya Pathak spoke about her bond with Mira Rajput Kapoor, saying she has never thought of her as a daughter-in-law but as part of the family. She also shared how her grandchildren, Misha and Zain, bring joy to their household.

Johnny Lever's daughter Jamie recalls brother Jessey's tumour battle at age 10 Jamie Lever recalled the difficult time when her brother Jessey Lever was diagnosed with a neck tumour at the age of 10. She shared how the family, including Johnny Lever, relied on faith as Jessey underwent two surgeries before he eventually recovered.

Rajpal Yadav celebrates 20 years of Malamaal Weekly, thanks fans for love The Priyadarshan comedy Malamaal Weekly was released 20 years ago on March 10. Rajpal Yadav, whose character Bajey gained immense popularity, thanked fans for all the love, sharing a compilation of his funniest scenes from the film.

Elijah Wood hints at his return as Frodo Baggins in The Hunt for Gollum Elijah Wood has said that he doesn't want anyone but himself playing Frodo Baggins in The Lord of the Rings: The Hunt for Gollum. Wood played Frodo in the previous instalments of the film series.

Pankaj Kapur says asking for script made him a difficult actor early in his career Pankaj Kapur said he was labelled a "difficult actor" early in his career because he insisted on reading the script before accepting roles.

One Piece Season 2 review: It gets bigger, stranger and mostly betterRelease Date:Tue Mar 10, 2026 One Piece Season 2 is finally streaming on Netflix. The series follows Monkey D Luffy and the Straw Hats as they enter the chaotic Grand Line, facing bizarre adventures and new allies.

Rashmika-Vijay's Pradhanam pics: All about her Shiva-Shakti mehndi and his style Rashmika Mandanna and Vijay Deverakonda shared pictures from their mehndi and pradhanam ceremony. Other than their moments from the pre-wedding festivities, it was Rashmika's beautiful mehndi that caught the attention of fans.

Ajith Kumar returns to Chennai after being stranded in Dubai amid US-Iran conflict The actor had reportedly been in Dubai when rising tensions in the UAE disrupted international flight routes, leaving travellers stranded.

No item song in Pawan Kalyan's Ustaad Bhagat Singh. Director explains why Director Harish Shankar says Ustaad Bhagat Singh will not feature a traditional item song, keeping in mind Pawan Kalyan's role as Andhra Pradesh Deputy Chief Minister.

Is Vijay-Rashmika's Ranabaali set amidst a deadly famine? Here's what we know Vijay Devarakonda and Rashmika Mandanna's upcoming drama Ranabaali is set against the backdrop of colonial rule in India. Read to know the details about the momentous historical events it sets out to capture.

Yash's Toxic is ready for release, no VFX delay: Source Rumours around the delay of Toxic: A Fairytale for Grownups claimed that Yash was unhappy with the film's VFX, but a source close to the production has denied the reports, calling them false.
Anil Ravipudi to team up with Jana Nayagan makers for animated film: Report Director Anil Ravipudi, known for commercial entertainers, is now reportedly mounting a large-scale animated film.

Harry Styles One Night in Manchester review: An ultimate fan concert at homeRelease Date:Mon Mar 09, 2026 Harry Styles: One Night in Manchester turned a packed stadium concert into an immersive streaming experience. Filmed at Manchester's Co-op Live arena, the special blends songs from his latest album, Kiss All the Time.

Saiyaara actor Aneet Padda in Madhubala biopic? Yash Raj Films clears the air While various names continue to circulate for the lead role in Madhubala biopic, Yash Raj Films has officially stepped in to silence the latest casting speculations. The production company also cleared the air on Aneet Padda's casting as Madhubala.

Jab Khuli Kitaab review: Pankaj Kapur leads tender story of love and its complexitiesRelease Date:Fri Mar 06, 2026 Jab Khuli Kitaab explores love, secrets and forgiveness when a woman reveals a decades-old infidelity to her husband while on her deathbed.

Randeep Hooda, Lin Laishram welcome baby girl: Lifetime of love Actors Randeep Hooda and Lin Laishram have welcomed their first child, a baby girl. The newborn shares her birthday with Randeep's father.

Did Timothee Chalamet's opera remark derail his Oscar dream? Controversy explained Timothee Chalamet, once considered a leading contender for Best Actor at this year's Oscars, now finds his prospects clouded by controversy over comments about ballet and opera. The actor has been nominated for his role in Marty Supreme.

Anupam Kher joins Prabhas in Fauzi, a 1940s drama on Azad Hind Force Veteran actor Anupam Kher has officially joined the cast of Prabhas-led Fauzi. The film, directed by Hanu Raghavapudi, is set against the backdrop of the 1940s Azad Hind Force.

Explained: Why BTS creator and HYBE boss Bang Si-hyuk faces trading violation probe Bang Si-hyuk, chairman of South Korean entertainment giant HYBE and mastermind behind the K-pop superband BTS, has been named to Billboard's 2026 Global Power Players list. Here's looking at the reasons he faces investigation over alleged unfair trading practices under the Capital Markets Act.

general news.headline: Deleted post, Iran denial: Did US Navy escort oil tanker through Hormuz?In a now deleted post on X, US Energy Secretary Chris Wright claimed the US Navy had escorted an oil tanker through the Strait of Hormuz to ensure global energy supplies continued flowing.

Live: US moving to dismantle Iran's missile-building infra, says White HouseUS-Israel Attacks Iran Live Updates: The Middle East war entered its 11th day with a dramatic escalation over global oil supplies. Iran's Revolutionary Guards warned they would not allow "one litre of oil" to leave the region if US and Israeli strikes continue, raising fears of a wider energy shock. US President Donald Trump responded with a stark threat, saying Washington would hit Iran "twenty times harder" if it blocked tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, which carries about a fifth of the world's oil. Markets have been rattled as tankers remain stranded and producers halt pumping, while Iran's security establishment appears to be rallying behind new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei amid signs the conflict could stretch longer than expected. Follow indiatoday.in for the latest updates on the Middle East crisis.

Air India fares to rise as Iran war pushes up jet fuel pricesAir India said the fuel surcharge is due to the sharp rise in aviation turbine fuel (ATF) prices since early March 2026, triggered by supply disruptions in the Gulf region.

India pumps 5,000 tonne of diesel into Bangladesh amid Iran war strainAmid war-driven energy strain, India has begun pumping 5,000 tonnes of diesel to Bangladesh through the cross-border Friendship Pipeline as part of an annual agreement to supply 180,000 tonnes of the fuel. The first consignment on Tuesday marks the start of the five-year-long supply plan, and could be seen as a sign of bilateral energy ties stabilising after Sheikh Hasina's exit.

Tees Maar Khan: Biography of Trump as the most dangerous fool in the worldHe is not a villain. He does not scheme. He simply acts, with total conviction, on a reality he invented. And the world keeps handing him a bigger sword, even when all he knows is how to squish flies.

India's submariners go deep. Their health data never surfacesHandcrafted ByOur Editors Sealed inside a steel tube at crushing depth, breathing manufactured air, eating off the same table where surgery will be performed if something goes wrong, India's submariners inhabit one of Earth's most hostile environments.

India eases FDI rules for China, allows up to 10% stake without govt approvalUnder the revised policy, overseas investors with up to 10% beneficial ownership from countries sharing a land border with India will now be allowed to invest through the automatic route.

Jaanbaz of Ramadan War: Is Mojtaba Khamenei wounded? Iran state TV drops hintAfter Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's killing in a US-Israeli strike, his son, Mojtaba Khamenei, was appointed the Supreme Leader. However, Mojtaba neither appeared in public nor delivered an address after that. The state media calling him a 'Jaanbaz of Ramadan War' has added to the buzz that he might have been injured in the strikes.

Dogs tied to poles, cats left behind: Expats flee Dubai without pets amid warA growing number of pets are being abandoned in Dubai as expatriates scramble to leave the region due to the escalating Middle East conflict.

Iran urges women footballers given asylum to come home: They killed schoolgirlsAustralia granted asylum to five members of Iran's women's football team, including captain Zahra Ghanbari, after they sought humanitarian protection. Tehran has urged the players to return home, while the case has drawn attention worldwide amid the Iran war.

Today will be the most intense day of strikes, US warns Iran Live UpdatesDefence Secretary Pete Hegseth said the US has been successful in degrading Iran's military capabilities, as the latter has fired the least number of missiles in the last 24 hours.

Why visit only temples? Kirti Azad's dig at Team India gets Harbhajan's comebackFormer cricketer Harbhajan Singh has responded to 1983 World Cup winner Kirti Azad's question - why do the Indian team always visit a temple, and not a mosque, or a church? Harbhajan's response has gone viral.

Rashmika-Vijay's mehendi outfits by Torani blend colour, tradition and boho charmRashmika Mandanna and Vijay Deverakonda's mehendi outfits designed by Karan Torani boasted a burst of colours and rich Indian craftsmanship.

Main Hoon Naa: IndiGo interim head's message to employees amid leadership changeRahul Bhatia expressed gratitude to employees who worked tirelessly to restore operations during that challenging period, praising their resilience and dedication.

Vikas Khanna hosts Zohran Mamdani for Iftar, remembers meeting him as a childChef Vikas Khanna hosted New York City's mayor Zohran Mamdani for Iftar at his Manhattan restaurant, reflecting on a connection that began when Mamdani was a child.

Don't believe he can live in peace: Trump's warning to Mojtaba KhameneiTrump said Iran "wants to talk badly" but warned the US would strike "20 times harder" if Tehran stops oil shipments through the Strait of Hormuz.

Is Vijay-Rashmika's Ranabaali set amidst a deadly famine? Here's what we knowVijay Devarakonda and Rashmika Mandanna's upcoming drama Ranabaali is set against the backdrop of colonial rule in India. Read to know the details about the momentous historical events it sets out to capture.

Aussie crew on US sub was sent to sleeping quarters before it sunk Iran shipLast week, Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese admitted that three navy personnel were on the US submarine that torpedoed and sank an Iranian warship in the Indian Ocean. It has now come to light what transpired on the submarine before the US attacked IRIS Dena.

Will India send warships to escort vessels amid Iran war? Pak navy already at itIndia is considering sending Navy ships to escort its fuel tankers stuck in the Strait of Hormuz amid the Iran war, according to reports. Pakistan is using naval escort for two vessels.

Academics call it the MIT of Middle East: The university that built Iran's engineersKnown widely as the "MIT of the Middle East," the Sharif University in the heart of Iran has trained generations of engineers who have gone on to shape industries across the world. Its reputation was built not only on rigorous selection and strong research but also on a culture that values problem-solving and innovation.

India now has over 50 living CheetahsThe birth marks a proud moment for Project Cheetah, which aims to restore the world's fastest land animal to Indian landscapes decades after it went extinct in the country.

How Iran war is forcing restaurants in Mumbai, Bengaluru, Chennai to shutThe ripple effect of the Iran conflict and the effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz, a vital global energy transit route, has led to an LPG cooking gas crunch in India. It has severely impacted the hospitality sector in Maharashtra, Tamil Nadu and Karnataka.

No LPG crisis, production up 10% today, says government, warns against fake newsGovernment sources said oil refineries have increased LPG production by around 10% following directions issued earlier and that additional administrative measures have been put in place to prevent hoarding and malpractice.

CBSE clarifies viral 'Rickroll' QR code in Class 12 maths paper, says exam security intactCBSE has clarified that the Class 12 mathematics board exam question papers are genuine and their security remains uncompromised after concerns emerged over a QR code linking to a YouTube video of 'Never Gonna Give You Up' by Rick Astley, a prank widely known online as "rickrolling".

sports.headline: ODI World Cup, WTC wins bigger than T20 World Cup: Manjrekar odd take goes viral Former cricketer Sanjay Manjrekar has gone viral over his odd take on the T20 World Cup. Manjrekar feels that ODI World Cup and the WTC wins are bigger achievements than winning the T20 World Cup.

England right-back Kyle Walker retires 4 games short of 100 international caps Kyle Walker has announced his retirement from international football, bringing the curtain down on a 14-year England career. Walker played for England 96 times and appeared at five major tournaments.

Frustrated with ICC, Windies board arranges flights for stranded players The West Indies Cricket Board is arranging commercial flights for its players and staff who continue to be stranded in India. The CWI has taken matter into its own hands after repeated delays by the ICC.

Rishabh Pant turns to Yuvraj Singh for career-revival in IPL Lucknow Super Giants captain Rishabh Pant is training under Yuvraj Singh ahead of the IPL 2026. Pant, who had a horrid time in the IPL last year, is hoping to revive his career in the T20 format.

Bangladesh vs Pakistan ODI series Live Streaming: Squads, all you need to know BAN vs PAK: Shaheen Shah Afridi's Pakistan and Mehidy Hasan Miraz's Bangladesh are all set to lock horns in a three-match ODI series to be held from March 11 at the Sher-e-Bangla National Stadium in Mirpur.

Australian GP Review: Dominant Mercedes, familiar Ferrari frustrations The 2026 Formula 1 season began with a thrilling Australian Grand Prix, but beneath Mercedes' dominant 1-2 finish were deeper questions about the new regulations, Ferrari's strategy woes, and a promising new rookie generation.

Watch: Sanju Samson's emotional reunion with family after dream T20 World Cup T20 World Cup: Sanju Samson shared an emotional reunion with his family in Trivandrum, thanking his parents and brothers for their unwavering support throughout his cricketing journey.

Why visit only temples? Kirti Azad's dig at Team India gets Harbhajan's comeback Former cricketer Harbhajan Singh has responded to 1983 World Cup winner Kirti Azad's question - why do the Indian team always visit a temple, and not a mosque, or a church? Harbhajan's response has gone viral.

Babar Azam's future in the dark? Pakistan focus on youngsters before ODI World Cup BAN vs PAK, 1st ODI: Shaheen Shah Afridi stated that Pakistan rested Babar Azam for the Bangladesh series to provide opportunities to youngsters, with next year's ODI World Cup in mind.

New Zealand's moment in the sun not far away T20 World Cup: The New Zealand men's team has played four ICC limited-overs finals since 2000 but has ended up on the losing side each time. On Sunday, the Kiwis were defeated by India by 96 runs in a one-sided final at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad.

Manisha Kalyan's world-class free-kick breathes life into Indian football Women's Asia Cup 2026: Manisha Kalyan's stunning free-kick gave India a brief moment of hope, but Chinese Taipei ran out 3-1 winners in Sydney, ending India's Women's Asia Cup campaign without a single point.

Sahibzada Farhan set for ODI debut after T20 WC surge, confirms Shaheen AfridiBAN vs PAK, 1st ODI: Shaheen Shah Afridi confirmed that Sahibzada Farhan, Shamyl Hussain, and Maaz Sadaqat will make their ODI debuts against Bangladesh in the series opener on Wednesday in Mirpur.

Watch: Ishan Kishan deflects question on Kirti Azad's controversial temple remarksIshan Kishan calmly avoided commenting on Kirti Azad's controversial remarks about taking the T20 World Cup trophy to a temple after India's victory. Instead, he urged journalists to focus on India's triumph and the team's memorable campaign.

Arshdeep fined for heated T20 World Cup final bust-up with Daryl MitchellFast bowler Arshdeep Singh has been reprimanded for his heated bust-up with New Zealand's Daryl Mitchell. In the T20 World Cup final, Arshdeep chucked a ball straight into the batter's body, in the 11th over of the game.

IPL 2026 schedule for first 20 matches to be announced by March 13: BCCIThe BCCI will unveil the schedule for the first 20 days of IPL 2026 by March 13, as confirmed by Secretary Devajit Sikia. The board is releasing fixtures in phases while factoring in state election dates.

Reminiscent of Viv Richards: Gavaskar's glowing verdict on Samson's World Cup heroicsT20 World Cup 2026: Sunil Gavaskar compared Sanju Samson to Viv Richards and Mahela Jayawardene after his Player of the Tournament performance at the T20 World Cup, praising his rare purity of batting.

Hell of a career: Starc pays special tribute to Alyssa Healy on Australia farewellMitchell Starc paid tribute to his wife Alyssa Healy after her international farewell, celebrating her remarkable career following Australia's Day-Night Test win over India at the WACA in Perth.

Delhi Capitals head coach Hemang Badani finds new role in The HundredHemang Badani has been appointed head coach of Southern Brave for the 2026 season of The Hundred, with Ian Bell serving as his assistant. The Brave will start their campaign on July 22 against Welsh Fire in Southampton.

West Indies, South Africa remain stranded in India after flight home gets cancelledT20 World Cup 2026: West Indies and South Africa cricketers remain stranded in Kolkata after their charter flight arranged to take them home was cancelled due to airspace clearance issues amidst the ongoing tensions in the West Asia region.

ICC call off Doha board meetings due to West Asia chaosThe ICC has called off its scheduled board and committee meetings in Doha due to escalating tensions in West Asia. Key discussions on broadcasting rights, the next FTP cycle and Olympic qualification will now be held virtually.

Jacob Bethell rewarded for T20 World Cup semis 105, named Birmingham Phoenix captainJacob Bethell has been named captain of Birmingham Phoenix for the 2026 season of the Hundred after his standout T20 World Cup campaign, highlighted by a 105 off 48 balls against India in the semi-final.

FIFA World Cup 2026 still on track in USA despite escalating West Asia CrisisFIFA says the 2026 World Cup in the USA will go ahead despite the ongoing conflict in West Asia, with Iran's participation in the tournament remaining uncertain due to the war.

UEFA Champions League Round of 16 preview: Key clashes to look out forThe UEFA Champions League Round of 16 is set to deliver big clashes as Europe's top clubs begin the knockout battle for Budapest. Heavyweights like Real Madrid, Manchester City, PSG and Barcelona headline the ties.

Wish you were here: Rinku Singh's emotional note for father after T20 World Cup winT20 World Cup: Rinku Singh shared an emotional note for his father on social media after India won the global event on Sunday. Last month, his father passed away following a prolonged battle with stage-4 liver cancer.

Gujarat Titans rope in Matthew Hayden as batting coach for IPL 2026IPL 2026: Gujarat Titans have brought in Australia great Matthew Hayden as batting coach ahead of IPL 2026. The former opener will work with captain Shubman Gill and the Titans' powerful batting unit to sharpen their approach.

Olympic award for India ex-captain for using volleyball as a gender levellerFormer India volleyball captain Abhijit Bhattacharya is the Global Winner of the Gender Equality, Diversity and Inclusion (GEDI) Champions Award 2025 given by the International Olympic Committee. Because of his efforts, thousands of boys and girls in rural Assam now play organised sport through the community-driven Brahmaputra Volleyball League.

BCCI announces Rs 131 crore for T20 World Cup 2026 winners. How it compares to 2024?T20 World Cup 2026: BCCI has announced Rs 131 crore for the T20 World Cup-winning side, surpassing the Rs 125 crore awarded in 2024, as India became the first team to defend the title.

Well played, coach sahab: Time Gautam Gambhir gets the credit he deservesT20 World Cup: Mocked, questioned and blamed for every stumble, Gautam Gambhir has quietly reshaped India's T20 identity. Now, with a World Cup in hand, the coach once doubted stands vindicated by a philosophy that turned India into the most ruthless T20 side in the world.

Coaches talked technique, but it was Dad's words that unlocked Abhishek SharmaAbhishek Sharma scored a crucial fifty in the T20 World Cup final after a string of poor performances. It was not his coaches but his father's simple words that turned his tournament around.

Neymar back in Brazil friendly squad: Big boost ahead of FIFA World CupNeymar has been included in Brazil's preliminary squad for friendlies against France and Croatia. But with Carlo Ancelotti closely monitoring his fitness, the Santos star faces a race against time to secure a place.

USA's Nathan Martin wins LA Marathon by 0.01 seconds, dramatic finish goes viralNathan Martin produced a stunning late surge to win the Los Angeles Marathon by 0.01 seconds. A viral video of rival Michael Kamau briefly running off course added drama to the race's closest finish.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
